title: Data Encryption
content: All sensitive patient data must be encrypted using strong encryption standards like AES-256.

title: Access Control
content: Access to patient data must be restricted based on user roles and permissions.

title: Unauthorized Access
content: Unauthorized access to healthcare systems can lead to data breaches and legal issues.

title: Data Breach
content: A data breach exposes confidential patient information to unauthorized parties.

title: Authentication
content: Multi-factor authentication should be implemented to enhance security.

title: Audit Logs
content: All activities in healthcare systems must be logged for auditing and compliance.

title: Weak Passwords
content: Weak passwords increase the risk of unauthorized system access.

title: Firewall Protection
content: Firewalls must be used to prevent unauthorized external access.

title: Data Privacy
content: Patient data must be handled in accordance with privacy regulations.

title: Network Security
content: Healthcare networks must be secured using encryption and monitoring tools.

title: Phishing Attacks
content: Phishing attacks can trick users into revealing sensitive healthcare data.

title: Backup Systems
content: Regular backups should be maintained to prevent data loss.

title: Data Retention
content: Healthcare data should be stored only for the required duration.

title: Malware Attacks
content: Malware can compromise healthcare systems and corrupt data.

title: Endpoint Security
content: All devices accessing healthcare systems must be secured.

title: VPN Usage
content: Secure VPN connections should be used for remote access.

title: Insider Threat
content: Employees misusing access can cause serious data leaks.

title: HIPAA Compliance
content: Healthcare systems must comply with HIPAA regulations for patient data protection.

title: Data Masking
content: Sensitive data should be masked when displayed to unauthorized users.

title: Unpatched Systems
content: Outdated software increases vulnerability to attacks.

title: Encryption in Transit
content: Data must be encrypted while being transmitted over networks.

title: Encryption at Rest
content: Stored data must be encrypted to prevent unauthorized access.

title: Ransomware
content: Ransomware attacks can lock healthcare data until payment is made.

title: Secure APIs
content: APIs must be secured using authentication and rate limiting.

title: Regulatory Standards
content: Healthcare systems must follow all applicable regulatory standards.

title: Data Leakage
content: Improper handling of data can lead to leakage.

title: User Monitoring
content: User activity must be monitored for suspicious behavior.

title: Session Management
content: Sessions must expire after inactivity.

title: Credential Theft
content: Attackers may steal login credentials.

title: Consent Management
content: Patient consent must be obtained before using data.

title: Access Logs
content: Maintain logs of all system access events.

title: Cloud Misconfiguration
content: Improper cloud setup can expose sensitive data.

title: Data Integrity
content: Ensure that healthcare data is not altered improperly.

title: Data Governance
content: Proper policies must govern healthcare data usage.

title: Third-party Risks
content: External vendors may introduce vulnerabilities.

title: Secure Storage
content: Data must be stored securely using encryption.

title: Denial of Service
content: DoS attacks can disrupt healthcare services.

title: Intrusion Detection
content: Systems must detect unauthorized access attempts.

title: Reporting
content: Security incidents must be reported promptly.

title: Human Error
content: Mistakes by staff can lead to data exposure.

title: Secure Coding
content: Applications must be developed following secure coding practices.

title: SQL Injection
content: Improper input handling can allow SQL injection attacks.

title: Input Validation
content: All inputs must be validated to prevent attacks.

title: Policy Enforcement
content: Security policies must be strictly enforced.

title: Cross-Site Scripting
content: XSS attacks can compromise user data.

title: Secure Authentication
content: Strong authentication mechanisms must be used.

title: Session Hijacking
content: Attackers may take control of user sessions.

title: Logging
content: Maintain detailed logs for troubleshooting and auditing.

title: Standards Compliance
content: Follow industry standards for healthcare systems.

title: Data Loss
content: Improper backups can lead to data loss.

title: Role-Based Access
content: Users should only access data relevant to their role.

title: System Downtime
content: Failures can disrupt healthcare services.

title: Encryption Keys
content: Keys must be securely stored and managed.

title: Audit Trails
content: Maintain detailed audit trails for compliance.

title: Unauthorized Devices
content: Unknown devices accessing systems pose risks.

title: Security Updates
content: Regular updates must be applied.

title: Outdated Protocols
content: Old protocols are vulnerable.

title: Secure Communication
content: Communication channels must be encrypted.

title: Legal Compliance
content: Follow all healthcare laws.

title: Improper Disposal
content: Improper data disposal can expose information.

title: Device Encryption
content: All devices must use encryption.

title: Lost Devices
content: Lost devices may expose data.

title: Biometric Security
content: Biometric authentication improves security.

title: Data Access Policies
content: Define clear data access rules.

title: Unauthorized Sharing
content: Sharing data without approval is risky.

title: Secure Login
content: Login systems must be secure.

title: Credential Reuse
content: Using same passwords increases risk.

title: Token Security
content: Tokens must be protected.

title: Confidentiality
content: Patient data must remain confidential.

title: Open Ports
content: Unnecessary open ports increase attack surface.

title: Patch Management
content: Apply patches regularly.

title: Brute Force Attacks
content: Attackers may try multiple passwords.

title: Rate Limiting
content: Limit requests to prevent abuse.

title: Documentation
content: Maintain proper documentation.

title: Zero-Day Attacks
content: Unknown vulnerabilities can be exploited.

title: Secure Hosting
content: Servers must be securely configured.

title: Data Exposure
content: Public exposure of data is dangerous.

title: Encryption Policies
content: Define strong encryption policies.

title: Security Training
content: Staff must be trained in security practices.

title: Social Engineering
content: Attackers manipulate users to gain access.

title: Threat Detection
content: Detect threats in real time.

title: Configuration Errors
content: Misconfigurations lead to vulnerabilities.

title: Secure Backups
content: Backups must also be encrypted.

title: Monitoring
content: Continuous monitoring is required.

title: Privilege Escalation
content: Attackers gain higher access levels.

title: Secure APIs
content: Protect APIs with authentication.

title: Insufficient Logging
content: Lack of logs makes detection difficult.

title: Access Reviews
content: Review user access regularly.

title: Incident Response
content: Prepare for handling security incidents.

title: Delayed Response
content: Slow response worsens damage.

title: Data Classification
content: Classify data based on sensitivity.

title: Shadow IT
content: Unauthorized systems increase risks.

title: Secure Storage Systems
content: Use secure storage solutions.

title: Risk Management
content: Identify and manage risks proactively.

title: Insufficient Security
content: Weak systems are easy targets.

title: Secure Architecture
content: Design systems with security in mind.

title: Unsecured APIs
content: APIs without protection expose data.

title: Monitoring Tools
content: Use tools to monitor system health.

title: Policy Updates
content: Update policies regularly.

title: Compliance Violations
content: Failure to follow regulations leads to penalties.
